# muxt check -v upgrades each error to the multi-line VerboseError form
# from github.com/typelate/check: in addition to the single-line summary,
# verbose mode includes the receiver type's declared-at position and (for
# named types whose source is readable) the Go source declaration of the
# type, godoc comment included.
#
# Without -v the error stays on one line. See err_check_with_wrong_field.txt
# for the non-verbose form.
! muxt check -v
stderr 'field or method Missing not found on Item'
stderr 'declared at .*main\.go:'
stderr 'Item is one row of the catalog\.'
stderr 'type Item struct'
stderr 'Title string'

# A plain `muxt check` keeps each error on a single line — verbose-only
# tokens like the rendered source block must not leak through.
! muxt check
! stderr 'type Item struct'
! stderr 'Item is one row of the catalog\.'

-- template.gohtml --
{{.Missing}}

-- go.mod --
module example.com

go 1.24
-- main.go --
package main

import (
	"embed"
	"html/template"
	"io"
)

//go:embed *.gohtml
var source embed.FS

var templates = template.Must(template.ParseFS(source, "*"))

// Item is one row of the catalog.
type Item struct {
	Title string
}

func main() {
	var d Item
	_ = templates.ExecuteTemplate(io.Discard, "template.gohtml", d)
}
